items.title: t-SNE
items.description: Both are non-linear visualization embedders emphasizing neighborhoods; t-SNE often prioritizes local clusters while UMAP typically retains more global layout. See t-SNE for perplexity and crowding-problem details—compare plots side by side rather than re-deriving t-SNE here.

items.title: Principal Component Analysis
items.description: PCA is the fast linear baseline that maximizes variance on centered data; use it first when axes and explained variance matter. UMAP targets curved manifold structure PCA cannot unfold.

items.title: Embedding visualization
items.description: UMAP outputs 2D/3D coordinates for scatter plots, color-by-label EDA, and interactive exploration—follow embedding-visualization practices for color, density, and overplotting.

items.title: k-Nearest Neighbors
items.description: UMAP constructs its high-dimensional graph from k-nearest-neighbor queries (n_neighbors controls k). See k-NN for distance metrics and search algorithms; UMAP owns how that graph becomes a fuzzy topology and embedding.

items.title: Scaling and normalization
items.description: Feature scaling before UMAP changes distance geometry—mixed units can dominate neighbor links unless features are standardized deliberately.

items.title: Curse of dimensionality
items.description: High-dimensional distances become less informative; non-linear embedders like UMAP help humans explore structure that raw high-D scatter cannot show.
